name: Downtown / Chinatown
desc: Business district by day, emerging arts/food scene by night — revitalized but still gritty, historic character.
insider: First Friday Chinatown art walk is the best free cultural event in Honolulu — galleries, food, live music, and street vibes
Maunakea Marketplace has the cheapest and most authentic Asian food in the city — Vietnamese pho, dim sum, and Filipino dishes
Keep your wits about you after dark on Hotel Street and River Street — open drug use and aggressive panhandling are real
The Hawaii State Art Museum (HiSAM) on Richards St is free, world-class, and almost never crowded
Iolani Palace is the only royal palace on US soil — tours are well worth the $25 and deeply moving
The Pig and the Lady on Smith Street is one of the best restaurants in all of Hawaii — Vietnamese-fusion at its finest
Lucky Belly does incredible ramen and Asian small plates — the belly bao is legendary among Honolulu foodies
Chinatown lei stands on Maunakea Street sell fresh leis for $5-15 — way cheaper than Waikiki gift shops
The neighborhood is in active transition — hip restaurants and galleries sit next to check-cashing shops and adult businesses
Hawaii Theatre on Bethel Street is a beautifully restored 1922 venue — catch a show for the architecture alone
